welcome.subtitle: I’m Baco — wine god and your personal sommelier. Ask me about any wine, send a label photo, or tell me your budget — I’ll handle it.
welcome.greetingFallback: Tell me what you’re looking for — I’ll handle it.

meta.title: Help — winegod.ai
meta.description: FAQs, glossary, and contact for winegod.ai.

header.title: Help
header.subtitle: Everything you need to use winegod.ai.

q1.a: It’s an AI assistant specialized in wine. You chat with Baco — the wine god — who answers about bottles, recommendations, comparisons, and more.

q2.intro: Anything about wine. A few examples:
examples: Compare Argentine Malbec and Chilean Carménère
Is this label any good? (with a photo)

q3.a: Baco replies in whatever language you write. English, Portuguese, Spanish, French, and others.

q1.q: Can I send a label photo?
q1.a: Yes. Snap the label and send it in the chat. Baco reads the image, identifies the wine, and pulls details from the database.

q2.q: What about shelf or menu photos?
q2.a: Also works. The system picks out the wines it can see. Sharp, well-lit photos give the best results.

q3.q: Can I send a PDF wine list?
q3.a: Yes. Baco extracts the wines listed in the PDF, looks each one up in the database, and replies with what it finds. Very long PDFs may be processed partially.

q1.a: A quality score on a 0 to 5 scale. It can come from public reviews or be estimated by the system when public data is sparse.

q2.a: A value-for-money index exclusive to winegod.ai. It blends quality, price, and micro-adjustments. Higher is better value.

q3.q: When Baco says “no rating,” what does that mean?
q3.a: It means the wine is in the database but lacks enough reviews for a confident rating. Baco does not invent ratings — if there isn’t one, he says so.

q1.q: How many messages can I send?
q1.a: Guests (no login) get 5 messages per session. Signed-in users get 15 messages per day, refreshed at midnight UTC.

q2.q: Do photos and PDFs cost more?
q2.a: A single photo is 1 credit. Sending 2–5 photos at once, a video, or a PDF costs 3 credits.

q3.q: How do I get more credits?
q3.a: Sign in with Google, Facebook, Apple, or Microsoft. You jump from 5 to 15 messages per day, refreshed automatically.

q1.q: Can I share a wine selection?
q1.a: Yes. When Baco lists wines, a share button appears. It generates a public link to the selection anyone can open without signing in.

q1.q: Do I need to create an account?
q1.a: No. You can use the chat as a guest. Signing in gets you more credits and, later, extra features.

q3.q: How do I delete my account?
q3.a: If you are signed in, you can delete your account directly on the Data deletion page. Email fallback is also available if you prefer.

baco.def: The winegod.ai character — the god of wine. He is the voice that answers your questions.

rotulo.def: The printed sticker on the bottle. It carries the wine name, producer, vintage, region, and alcohol content.

safra.def: The year the grapes were harvested. Not every wine has a vintage (non-vintage wines blend multiple years).

terroir.def: The combination of climate, soil, and local practice that gives a wine from a specific place its unique character.

tanino.def: A compound from grape skins, seeds, and stems. It gives red wine its drying grip and structure.

corpo.def: The perceived weight of the wine in the mouth. It can be light, medium, or full.

acidez.def: The wine’s freshness. Balanced acidity makes a wine feel lively and bright.

secoDoce.def: Dry = no perceptible residual sugar. Sweet = noticeably sweet. In between there are off-dry and semi-sweet styles.

blend.def: A wine made from more than one grape variety. For example, Cabernet Sauvignon + Merlot.

varietal.def: A wine made predominantly from a single grape variety. For example, 100% Malbec.

decantacao.def: Pouring wine from the bottle into a decanter to let it breathe and leave sediment behind.

ocr.def: Optical Character Recognition. The technology winegod.ai uses to read text in photos and PDFs.

wineGodScore.def: The winegod.ai value-for-money index. Blends quality and price on a 0 to 5 scale.

notaWCF.def: Our internal pure-quality rating. It is the base for the WineGod Score calculation.

credito.def: The chat usage unit. Each message costs 1 or more credits depending on the attached media.

produtor.def: The winery or company that makes the wine. For example, Catena Zapata, Penfolds.

contact.title: Contact
contact.intro: For questions, suggestions, or issues, email privacy@winegod.ai.
